source_files: Daily Cash Fcst - 1.21.25_BU view_deck version_working_Apr append_deck2_JanBOD.xlsx
Daily Cash Fcst - 12.30.25_BU view_deck version final.xlsx
Daily Cash Fcst - 01.16.26_BU view new format_asset sales_JanBOD.xlsx

re_and_debt_init_with_manteno_sheet.description: Liquidity pro forma sheet created for the Dec 2022 debt exchange. Tracks daily liquidity impact under two scenarios (Debt exchange closes 12/30/22 vs post-12/30). Contains Manteno property closing events (GR disbursements submitted 12/9, 12/14, 12/22; closing 12/29/2022) and Delano closing (1/5/2023). Also models a UBS loan upsize of $63M on 1/11/2023 and interest savings from debt exchange. Sheet has been carried forward unchanged in all subsequent files as a historical reference.

key_events.action: UBS Loan Upsize + Operating Reserve Rebalance

trapped_cash_and_bohemia.description: Tracks restricted cash unavailable for operations, categorized by: restricted cash (LCs, segregated accounts), accounts receivable/other assets, cash and cash equivalents
trapped_cash_and_bohemia.note: Lacey restricted cash cleared to $0 by Jan 2026; Bohemia $5.05M and Hackensack $0.57M remain restricted as of Jan-Mar 2026

asset_sales_proceeds_tracker.description: Column 68 in Cash Flow sheet: 'Proceeds from Asset Sales ($250M Balance)'. Tracks cumulative asset sale proceeds against a $250M contractual obligation. Balance hit $250M cap in June 2022. Post-cap inflows are incremental and tracked separately.

2020_pre_bankruptcy_legacy.description: Includes Innovel supply chain sale (~$930M Mar 2020), Kenmore/DieHard brand sales (~$89M), store liquidation proceeds. These are pre-reorganization events tracked in the same column.

notable_events.note: Large bulk sale brought balance to $249.9M

post_cap_proceeds_by_year.description: Proceeds received AFTER $250M balance cap was hit. These flow through the system but the balance tracker stays at $250M.

bohemia_ny.note: Bohemia generates ~$355K/month revenue (rental income). Loan has not been reduced as of May 2026. $5.05M remains restricted.

jan_31_2025_event.description: Major restructuring - column shows -$390.854M as netting adjustment. Corresponds to LeaseOpCo loan dropping from $827.4M to $436.6M on the same date.

lacey_wa.status: Column used as netting/flow account for UBS facility draws. $11.2M refers to the facility size on the Lacey WA property.
lacey_wa.note: The column shows large positive and negative values representing draws and repayments through the UBS Lacey facility - not the actual property value

new_brunswick_nj.status: Being paid down - $13.5M reduced to $4.25M (69% paid off)
new_brunswick_nj.note: Steady amortization from $13.5M to $4.25M over ~13 months

hackensack_nj_2022.loan_name: Hackensack, NJ Loan (2022) / Grant Street Funding
hackensack_nj_2022.status: Static - collateralized by Safelite rent reserve
hackensack_nj_2022.note: Small loan secured by Safelite rent payments. Balance unchanged at $7.585M since Oct 2022.

hackensack_nj_citi_manteno_2022.loan_name: CITI/Manteno Loan → UBS/Manteno Loan → Hackensack NJ Loan (2026)
hackensack_nj_citi_manteno_2022.evolution: Initially labeled 'CITI/Manteno Loan', then 'UBS/Manteno Loan' from 2024, renamed 'Hackensack NJ Loan (2026)' in May 2026
hackensack_nj_citi_manteno_2022.status: ACTIVELY BEING RETIRED - 88% paid off from peak

manteno_il.description: Distribution Center / industrial property in Manteno, IL
manteno_il.re_debt_init_sheet: RE & Debt init with Manteno - historical liquidity pro forma
manteno_il.notes: Manteno property was monetized at closing Dec 29, 2022. The $67M loan was secured against Manteno collateral and has been paid down by 88% as of May 2026. Gotion received 3rd of 3 payments per cash file details ($0.63M Manteno/Gotion payment).

durham_nc.status: NEW - added to CF tracker July 2025. Represents a new property monetization.
durham_nc.note: First appears in Jul 2025. This is a new loan/monetization event not present in pre-2025 files.

propco_lease_opco_structure.description: PropCo / LeaseOpCo is the PropHoldco entity that holds properties leased to operating entities. LeaseOpCo loan tracks the debt balance against this portfolio.

lease_opco_loan_balance_history_M.event: Large drawup - UBS loan facility expansion
MAJOR RESTRUCTURING: -$390.8M in single day. Corresponds to UBS portfolio rebalancing.

propco_cash_flows_from_cash_file_details.description: Monthly recon file with PropCo for 3PL EBITDA (Lawrence & Morrisville), mgmt fees, etc.

propco_2025_separate_line.description: Separate line added in 2025 for UBS property tax payments pre-1/31/2025 proration
propco_2025_separate_line.note: Expected to be zero in FY26 - only mgmt fees going forward

real_estate_transactions_by_year.description: RE Transactions column (col 101 in Jan 2026) captures lease termination settlements, MOAC litigation costs, and other one-time RE cash flows (outflows negative)

jan_2026_strategic_shift.file: Daily Cash Fcst - 01.16.26_BU view new format_asset sales_JanBOD.xlsx
jan_2026_strategic_shift.significance: First time 'asset sales' appears in the file name itself, signaling a strategic pivot to explicit asset monetization as a primary liquidity lever
structural_changes_from_prior_files: File renamed to include 'new format' and 'asset sales' for first time
Durham NC Loan added as new column (col 65) - represents new property monetization active since Jul 2025
PropCo 2025 separated as distinct line item (col 103) alongside PropCo (col 110)
Column 137: 'Cash Flows no Asset Sales' explicitly added - management now tracking operating CF independently from asset monetization
RE Fcst Summary 1.16.26 added as a new detailed sheet (previously was RE Fcst Summary 12.10.24)
Hackensack NJ restricted cash ($570K) and Bohemia restricted cash ($5.05M) specifically itemized in Trapped Cash
capex forecast 1.16.26 sheet shows $36.7M FY26 capex for PH2.0 portfolio and $5M for Bohemia
jan_2026_strategic_shift.interpretation: The Jan 2026 'new format' represents management explicitly repositioning the company around RE asset monetization as the primary path forward. The addition of 'Cash Flows no Asset Sales' column suggests awareness that operating CF alone is insufficient and asset sales are now a planned, systematic (not opportunistic) source of liquidity.

monetization_pace_analysis.summary: Asset sale proceeds have been highly variable, driven by lumpy individual transactions rather than a systematic disposal program. The $250M tracker cap (reached Jun 2022) relates to a contractual obligation, not the total proceeds available.
monetization_pace_analysis.pre_2022_phase: Legacy divestiture - Innovel, brands, store-level assets from bankruptcy proceedings
monetization_pace_analysis.2022_2023_phase: Property-level disposals: Manteno IL (Dec 2022), Delano, smaller store terminations and settlements

2024_acceleration.note: 2024 was the peak post-cap year. The $34.5M event on 3/12/2024 confirmed in Inflows Actuals. Corresponds to -$25M LeaseOpCo reduction on 3/13 suggesting partial UBS portfolio monetization.

2025_deceleration.note: Slower pace. No Q1 2025 asset sales. Q4 2025 had $23.2M driven by Oct 17, 2025 $15.45M event. Oct event correlates with -$15.5M LeaseOpCo reduction suggesting another UBS/PropCo property sale.

2026_ytd_pace.note: Three events: Jan 30 $4.94M, Feb 13 $5.57M, Feb 27 $2.09M. No March-May actuals. Hackensack 2026 loan shows $7.975M on May 1 (from $20.5M Dec 2024) - suggesting ~$12.5M in paydowns applied, consistent with asset sale proceeds being used to retire this loan.

hackensack_2026_paydown_trajectory.note: This $12.5M paydown aligns with the $12.6M in 2026 YTD asset sale proceeds - strong evidence that asset sales are being directed to Hackensack loan retirement
